Operation 'post_/logout' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Current description is too informal and lacks important context. Improved version clarifies authentication requirement, explains the side effect (session termination), and specifies that the token becomes invalid. This helps AI agents understand the operation's impact on subsequent requests.

data.current_description: Log out by deleting your token off the server.
data.description_suggestion: Invalidates the current authentication token by removing it from the server. Requires a valid TokenHeader. After logout, subsequent requests without a new token will be rejected. This operation terminates the authenticated session.
data.reasoning: Current description is too informal and lacks important context. Improved version clarifies authentication requirement, explains the side effect (session termination), and specifies that the token becomes invalid. This helps AI agents understand the operation's impact on subsequent requests.

data.current_description: Returns a list of signin objects sorted by signin ID descending.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of signin records sorted by signin ID in descending order. Requires authentication. Supports filtering and pagination parameters. Returns an array of signin objects containing attendee information and timestamps. Useful for contact tracing and attendance tracking.

Description lacks information about parameters (2 parameters mentioned but not explained), authentication requirement, and use case context. Improved version clarifies the operation retrieves attendee signin records, mentions pagination/filtering support, and provides domain context for AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Used for resetting your password when you forgot it
data.current_description: Another endpoint will generate a password reset code for you. You should use the client app to submit the reset code along with the new password to change your password.
data.summary_suggestion: Verify and apply password reset with code
data.description_suggestion: Completes the password reset process by verifying the password reset code and applying the new password. Requires the reset code (generated by /requestPasswordReset) and the new password as inputs. Returns confirmation of successful password change. This endpoint is used after an admin generates a reset code for a user who forgot their password.
data.reasoning: Original summary conflates 'forgot password' with the verification step. Description lacks clarity on inputs (reset code, new password), outputs, and the relationship to the requestPasswordReset endpoint. Added specific input/output details and clarified the workflow for AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'post_/requestPasswordReset' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear but description contains implementation rationale that obscures the operation's purpose and interface. Removed implementation details about SMTP and adoption ease, focusing instead on inputs, outputs, and the workflow. Clarified the manual code delivery process and relationship to verifyPasswordChange endpoint for AI agent clarity.

data.current_summary: Used for requesting a password reset code
data.current_description: The admin should run this on behalf of a user who forgot their password. The API will generate a password reset code which the admin should then provide to the user. The user can use their client to reset their password. Normally the password reset code is mailed to the user, but I didn't want to do this in this case because I didn't want to introduce the complicated dependency of having an SMTP server just for this purpose. Doing it this way makes it easy for people to adopt this API.
data.description_suggestion: Generates a password reset code for a user who forgot their password. Requires the user ID as input. Returns a password reset code that the admin must manually provide to the user. The user then uses this code with the /verifyPasswordChange endpoint to set a new password. Note: The reset code is not automatically sent via email; the admin is responsible for communicating it to the user.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description contains implementation rationale that obscures the operation's purpose and interface. Removed implementation details about SMTP and adoption ease, focusing instead on inputs, outputs, and the workflow. Clarified the manual code delivery process and relationship to verifyPasswordChange endpoint for AI agent clarity.
